top_categories.workout_context: 6,562ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Granfondo Strade Bianche's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before March.
At mile 60 of 86, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
76ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Granfondo Strade Bianche's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 86 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Granfondo Strade Bianche. Train it until it feels boring.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 6,562ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 86 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 86 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Granfondo Strade Bianche, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
At Granfondo Strade Bianche, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Granfondo Strade Bianche’s 86 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Granfondo Strade Bianche. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 86 miles of mixed terrain in Siena, Tuscany, Italy.
